title: get error message "Unable to log in at server. Probably wrong configuration, username or password." when I try to install Thunderbird on new WQindows 11 computer
creator: Henry56
content:
I had Thunderbird on my Windows 10 computer. I bought a new Windows 11 computer and downloaded Thunderbird. When I try to set up my email account, I provide my full name (not my Thunderbird user name) and email address. The next screen has the message "Configuration found in Mozilla ISPDB ". I clicked on IMAP and hit continue. The next screen I type in my password for my Spectrum mail account. I have confirmed that the password works went I log onto the Spectrum website. The next screen has the Authentication error "Unable to log in at server. Probably wrong configuration, username or password.". I've also tried using my Thunderbird user name and Password instead of my full name and Spectrum password with the same results. I know my Thunderbird user name is correct but I can't be 100% sure that my Thunderbird password is correct.

I'm not sure if this matters but my email has the has the extension ----@socal.rr.com. This was assigned to me by my original email provider, which was Time Warner Cable. Spectrum bought Time Warner Cable a few years ago and I think Spectrum uses a different extension.

title: request.
creator: jackinandroidon
content:
I have a request.

Please make it easier to send feedback to the developers.

I don’t want to have to log in to the forum every time just to ask someone who might not even be an expert.

I’m Japanese and don’t speak English, so the English forum is difficult for me to use.

It’s a hassle to have to post on the forum like this just because I can’t easily send feedback in my local language.

I have two pieces of feedback I wanted to share this time.

1. Sometimes when I open Thunderbird, I get an error message saying it can’t load because there are too many accounts. Is this because it’s trying to check them all at once?

Please have it load them one by one in order. Or, please add an option in the settings to do this.

2. Messages marked with “Re:” are replies, right?

Let’s say my account is “Apple.” If I reply from “Apple,” a message marked “Re:” should remain in the “Apple” folder. From the recipient’s perspective, replies to emails from “Apple” should also come from “Apple.”

However, there were emails where I was recorded as having replied from a different account, even though I don’t recall switching accounts when replying.

So, from the recipient’s perspective, it looked like the email came from a stranger, and they replied asking me to identify myself.

While it’s not impossible that I might have changed the address by mistake while half-asleep, I’m reporting that there were instances where I was recorded as replying from a different address even though I don’t remember making a mistake.

I just want to share a request and a report, so there’s no need to reply.

Translated with DeepL.com (free version)

要望です。

開発者にフィードバックを簡単に送れるようにしてください。

いちいちフォーラムへログインして、素人かもしれない人に訊きたい訳ではありません。

私は日本人で英語が出来ませんので、英語のフォーラムも使いにくいです。

簡単にローカル言語でフィードバックが送れないから、こうしてフォーラムで投稿しないといけないのは手間です。

今回送りたかったフィードバックは二つです。

1、サンダーバードを開くと、アカウントが多いからロード出来ないとかエラーメッセージが出てくることが時々ありますが、一度に確認しようとするからでしょう？

一つずつ順番に読み込むようにしてください。 もしくは、設定で、そのように出来るようにしてください。

2、Reが付いているものは、返信したものでしょう？

アカウントがリンゴだとします。 リンゴから返信したら、リンゴの中にRe付きのものが残るはずです。 相手からしたら、リンゴから来たメールの返信もリンゴから来るはずです。

しかし、返信する時にアカウントを変えた覚えがないのに、違うアカウントから返信したことになったメールがありました。

なので、相手からしたら、知らない人からメールが来たことになり、誰だか分からないから教えてくださいと返信が来ました。

ねぼけて返信時にアドレスを変更した可能性も0ではありませんが、間違えた覚えが無いのに違うアドレスから返信したことになったものがあったことを報告しておきます。

リクエストと報告を伝えたいだけなので、返信は不要です。

content: Thunderbird has been acting weird. For a week or so, it has sometimes been downloading only one message at a time when I hit "Get Messages." I have to keep clicking "Get Messages" over and over to download my email. Now, however, it won't retrieve any messages at all from that account. The online information tells me to delete my account and then add it again, but it also seems to indicate that if I do that I'll lose the email that is currently stored under that account. That's not an option! I am able to send messages, and Thunderbird retrieves messages from my other email account just fine.

title: Reminder snooze options "x minutes before start"
creator: firefox1386
content:
Is there a setting, like in Outlook, to snooze a reminder until "x minutes before start"?

If I have a reminder for e.g. 3pm, which is set to go off 1 hour before and then I want to snooze to give 5 minutes warning, in Outlook I would set it to remind me 5 minutes before. This works at any time up until 6 minutes before the event, regardless of when I snooze it..

But it seems that in TB, if you're away from your desk until e.g. 2.30pm, then you would have to calculate and manually enter "remind me in 25 minutes" to achieve the same effect.

I can't find an addon which does this, but happy to stand corrected!

title: unable to delete
creator: marianne30
content:
In my Thunderbird I have some folders with names printed italic starting with "INBOX". The folders are empty, so it's easy to delete them. After deleting I don't see the folders anymore. But after shutting down Thunderbird and starting Thunderbird again, the folders are back... How do I delete the folders to not see them anymore?

Best regards, Marianne
metadata: ;useragent:Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Ubuntu; Linux x86_64; rv:151.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/151.0;tb_version:140.11.0esr;os:Linux

title: How do I allow Mozilla Thunderbird to access my google account
creator: abbateandy
content: A page appears when I open Thunderbird asking for permission to allow Mozilla Thunderbird to access my google account. It asks for me to click on "allow" but nothing happens and the page keeps appearing.
metadata: ;useragent:Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/148.0.0.0 Safari/537.36;tb_version:151.0.1;os:Windows 11

title: Thunderbird profile transfer problem
creator: hclase
content:
I am setting up a new installation of KDE6 on PClinuxOS and have hit a problem with copying over my Thunderbird profile from my old KDE5 installation.

I keep the profiles for Firefox and Thunderbird on a separate data partition and access them from my home folder with symbolic links.

I then just copy the whole thing over to another computer's data file linked in the same way. This has always worked in the past and Firefox copied over OK, but there's a small problem with Thunderbird, everything is there but the inbox. I get the message:- 

  " Unable to open the summary file for inbox on MUN. Perhaps there was an error on disk, or the full path is too long."

(MUN is the name of the mail server)

I've recopied several times but the problem persists. I'm not sure what file it's referring to, there is nothing by that name that I can find. 

The outbox is OK I've just sent myself a message.
metadata: ;useragent:Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Linux x86_64; rv:151.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/151.0;tb_version:148.0.1 to 151.0.1;os:PCLinux OS

title: when trying to access my email on thunderbird, I get the message: " Login to server mail.sasktel.net with username powalinsky failed".
creator: powalinsky
content: I get the message "Login to server mail.sasktel.net with username powalinsky failed" even though none of the sign-in credentials have changed and I can access the same account in a browser and on my iphone. This problem began last Friday. It has occurred occasionally and briefly in the past, seemingly resolving itself. I have tried the troubleshooting suggestions that I found online, but to no avail.
metadata: ;useragent:Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64; rv:151.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/151.0;tb_version:151.0.1;os:Windows 11

title: Copy text (Ctrl-C or context menu) not working
creator: wdburgdorf
content:
This is actually the same in Firefox, and both only since today, working fine before:

Typing Ctrl-C does nothing, Ctrl-X deletes whatever is selected, but it does not end up in the clipboard. In fact, if the clipboard contains something, it will be removed (Win-V still shows it, but it can no longer be pasted). When copying from somewhere else, pasting with Ctrl-V works.

AFAICT, no issues with any other programs.

Latest versions of FF (151.0.2) and Thunderbird (151.0.1), on Windows 10.

What could be wrong?

Thanks, Ralf
metadata: ;useragent:Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/148.0.0.0 Safari/537.36;tb_version:151.0.1;os:Windows 10

title: orange refuses to connect to thunderbird
creator: schmid.sigrid
content: I use thunderbird for a long time. all of a sudden, I can see the addresses of the mails that have arrived, but I cannot open them - I get the message "orange (imap.orange.fr) is unable to connect to thunderbird". I cannot send anything - message "close the program or wait for the program to respond" - I wait for a long time, a little circle keeps turning - that's all. the mail-orange functions without problems, but I prefer thunderbird. what can I do to re-establish the connection orange-thunderbird? thanks - sigrid
metadata: ;useragent:Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/148.0.0.0 Safari/537.36 Edg/148.0.0.0;tb_version:can't;os:Windows 11

title: I cannot see any new email messages. Thunderbird only show mail form ten years ago.
creator: bwschafer1
content:
I have recently installed Thunderbird. I have imported the .pst from outlook. I cannot see any email sent recently. Email display Window only shows mail messages from ten years ago. When I select get messages it states no new messages to download What do I need to do to fix this ?

Thank you Brad
